commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh I have surely heard Ephraim bemoaning himself thus; You have chastised me, and I was chastised, as a bullock unaccustomed to the yoke: restore me, and I shall return; for you are the LORD my God. Chaldean, Theodoret, and St. Chrysostom have "not. "But chastisement produced a salutary effect upon the ten tribes, Osee vi. Bring me back from captivity and from my evil ways. This must be attributed to grace. (Calmet) We cannot repent without it. Jerome) Da quod jubes. Augustine, Confessions x. 19, 31, and 37. ) God's grace is the principal cause of justification. Man's co-operation is the secondary cause. (Worthington).
